user_query:
I'm launching a dual-platform content push for a budget cordless stick vacuum aimed at small apartments. Read input/brief.json and input/EXTEND.md, then create two files:
1) output/plan.md — a complete dual-platform plan following this strict structure and order:
- "## 📸 视觉方案" with fields for 画面描述, 构图, 色调 (3 hex colors), 封面文字 (put the cover text in double quotes and keep it ≤8 Chinese characters), and an AI绘图提示词 code block
- "## ✍️ 小红书文案" with 标题 (≤20 Chinese characters), 正文 (≤800 Chinese characters, colloquial with emojis, at least two Chinese modal particles like 啦/呀/哦/嘛, and at least one personal anecdote), 标签 (5–10 tags), 发布时间 (a specific slot with a short reason)
- "## 📌 Pinterest Copy" with English Title (≤100 characters), English Description (≤500 characters with main keywords in the first 40 chars and a soft CTA), Board (keyword-rich), and Post Time (UTC slot + reason)
- "## 🎯 运营动作" with 首条自评, 回复模板 (3 variations, numbered 1–3), 系列规划 (at least 下一篇 and 第三篇), and 真人感检查清单 (at least 5 checklist items)
- "## Character Count Verification" — a table listing Cover text, XHS title, Pinterest title, and Pinterest description with Limit, Actual, and Pass columns
Make sure the Xiaohongshu copy is in Chinese and follows the platform specs, and the Pinterest copy is in natural English with a search-first angle. Use the brand colors and posting cadence from the preferences, choose non-on-the-hour times, and explicitly include anti-AI tactics across copy, visuals, behavior, and comments.
2) output/diagnosis.md — before the strategy, write 1–3 bullet points identifying fatal weaknesses in the initial idea (hook, visuals, platform fit, AI smell, etc.), each followed by a concise fix.
If anything critical is missing from the brief, ask one clarifying question before proceeding.
